capability_objective: Plan and execute independent lookups in parallel, gate dependent retrievals on uniquely resolved stable IDs, respect read-only negative constraints, and synthesize an exact-format comparison from full records.
prompt: The operations coordinator is assembling the meeting-status section of the regional brief. The brief needs evidence from full records rather than search snippets. Find the meeting named “Quarterly Roadmap Review” at “Denver HQ” and the meeting named “Budget Close Readout” at “Chicago Hub”. Use the genuine local meeting client at `./meetingctl`; its built-in help describes its executable interface. First issue both name-and-location searches concurrently in one assistant action. After both results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both full-record retrievals concurrently in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. When both branches resolve, write the finished meeting-status section to `regional-brief.md`. It must contain exactly three Markdown lines beginning `- `: one per record in the requested order, then one status-comparison bullet. Each record bullet must include that meeting's name, location, stable ID, and status. The comparison bullet must include both status values and state whether they match or differ. Do not include headings, blank lines, or additional prose in that file.
